phrases.intent_en: Ask where the fish market is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le marché aux poissons, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV leh mar-SHAY oh pwah-SOHN, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: The fish market (la criée) runs every morning on the quayside — arrive before 9am for the best selection. It's been Marseille's fishing hub for centuries.

responses.english: On the Quai des Belges, right there. Open in the morning.
responses.phonetic: say par lah, zhoost oh boo duh lah roo
responses.french: Sur le quai des Belges, juste là. Ouvert le matin.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the navette boat taxi to Frioul
phrases.text_local: Comment prendre la navette pour les îles du Frioul ?
phrases.pronunciation: kuh-MAN PRAN-dreh lah nah-VET poor layz EEL doo free-OOL
phrases.cultural_note: The navette (shuttle boat) runs every 15-20 minutes to the Frioul islands. A round trip costs about €8.50 and takes 10 minutes.

responses.english: At the Quai des Belges, about every twenty minutes.
responses.phonetic: toot lay kah-NEEZ ah vahn mee-NOOT
responses.french: Au Quai des Belges, toutes les vingt minutes environ.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a recommendation for a bouillabaisse restaurant
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me recommander un restaurant où l'on sert une bonne bouillabaisse ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo meh reh-ko-man-DAY un res-to-RAN oo luhn sehr oon bon boo-yah-BES
phrases.cultural_note: True bouillabaisse is a complex dish — expect to pay €25-40 for a proper serving. The traditional version uses at least five types of Mediterranean fish.

responses.english: Ask the locals, there are several good places around the port.
responses.phonetic: zhuh ray-ko-MAHN-duh luh spay-see-ah-lee-TAY loh-KAL, say tek-seh-LAHN
responses.french: Demandez aux locaux, il y a plusieurs bonnes adresses autour du port.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the ferry to Château d'If departs
phrases.text_local: D'où part le ferry pour le Château d'If ?
phrases.pronunciation: doo PAR leh FEH-ree poor leh shah-TOH deef
phrases.cultural_note: Ferries to Château d'If depart from the Vieux-Port. The 15-minute crossing offers views of the fortress made famous by Alexandre Dumas' 'The Count of Monte Cristo'.

responses.english: From the Quai de la Fraternité, in front of the City Hall.
responses.phonetic: say par lah, zhoost oh boo duh lah roo
responses.french: Du quai de la Fraternité, devant l'Hôtel de Ville.

phrases.cultural_note: Pastis is the quintessential Marseille drink — an anise-flavored aperitif. Add water to cloud it. It's traditionally served with ice and a glass of water.

responses.phonetic: byan sewr, zhuh voo ah-PROHNT sah too deh SWEET
